forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system east of Tasmania will maintain a ridge over SA this week. A cold front is expected to weaken and slide across western and southern coasts during today and Wednesday. A second cold front may pass over the south again on Saturday with a high pressure system to follow over waters south of the Bight on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly during the day. Winds reaching up to 20 knots near the Yorke Peninsula coast during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres near the Yorke Peninsula coast around midday.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to around 1 metre south of Cowell to Wallaroo.

Northwest to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots near the Yorke Peninsula coast early in the morning. Winds tending southeast to southwesterly in the middle of the day then tending southeasterly in the evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to around 1 metre south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
East to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning north to northeasterly during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the morning.
